skill.name: api-documentation-generator
skill.description: Generates OpenAPI and Swagger documentation from REST API route files, with guidance for Express, FastAPI, Flask, NestJS, and Rails projects.
skill.summary: Generate OpenAPI documentation from existing REST API routes.
skill.author: CrazyDubya

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL patterns, but review found these are documentation and template false positives. No executable shell commands, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious network behavior were found. The remaining risk is limited to intended file writes for generated OpenAPI documentation.

low_findings.title: Intended Documentation File Writes
low_findings.description: The skill is allowed to write and edit files so it can create or update OpenAPI documentation. This is expected behavior, and the instructions limit writes to documentation outputs such as openapi.yaml, docs, or api directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write capability is explicit in allowed tools and output instructions. It appears legitimate for this documentation generator, with no evidence of hidden or destructive writes.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution and weak cryptography matches occur in Markdown fences, route syntax, OpenAPI references, descriptions, and example schemas. They are not executable commands or cryptographic implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation, examples, or YAML template text. No runtime code path executes these snippets or implements cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs in OpenAPI Template
low_findings.description: The template includes example license and server URLs. These are static documentation placeholders and do not cause network requests by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside an OpenAPI YAML template. There is no code that fetches, posts, or transmits data to those URLs.

content.value_statement: API documentation often falls behind route changes and framework-specific conventions. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code inspect routes and produce consistent OpenAPI or Swagger documentation.
actual_capabilities: Finds common REST route patterns in Express, FastAPI, Flask, NestJS, and Rails projects.
Extracts HTTP methods, paths, parameters, request bodies, responses, and authentication hints.
Creates or updates OpenAPI 3.0 documentation from a provided YAML template.
Maps route comments, docstrings, DTOs, and schemas into endpoint descriptions where available.
Adds common error responses, tags, security schemes, and request or response examples.
Suggests an output location such as openapi.yaml, docs, or api when project structure is clear.
limitations: It relies on readable route files and may miss dynamically registered routes.
Generated schemas need review when request and response types are implicit.
It does not run API servers or validate live endpoint behavior.
It may need user guidance for unusual frameworks or custom routing layers.

use_cases.title: Document an Existing REST Service
use_cases.description: Generate a first OpenAPI specification from route files, comments, and framework conventions.

use_cases.title: Update API Docs Before Release
use_cases.description: Refresh endpoint paths, request bodies, responses, and authentication details before publishing a version.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Documentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent tags, response formats, and schema references across several service modules.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic API Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate OpenAPI documentation for this project. Find the route files, identify endpoints, and create an openapi.yaml file.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Specification
prompt_templates.scenario: Refresh an existing OpenAPI file after route changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current route files with the existing OpenAPI document. Update missing endpoints, parameters, responses, and tags.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Schema Accuracy
prompt_templates.scenario: Use local types and models to improve generated schemas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate OpenAPI docs and use available DTOs, TypeScript interfaces, Pydantic models, or validators for request and response schemas.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Completeness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before publishing API documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the API documentation for completeness. Check every route, authentication requirement, error response, schema reference, and example.

output_examples.input: A Node.js project with Express routes for users and authentication.
output_examples.output: A generated OpenAPI document with user and authentication tags, path parameters, request bodies, response descriptions, and bearer authentication.

output_examples.input: A FastAPI service with Pydantic models and route decorators.
output_examples.output: An enhanced documentation plan that reuses model names, records status codes, and adds clearer endpoint descriptions and examples.

output_examples.input: A Rails API with namespaced routes and controllers.
output_examples.output: A route-based API documentation draft covering resource endpoints, nested paths, common errors, and grouped tags.

best_practices: Review generated schemas against source models before publishing documentation.
Keep examples realistic but remove secrets, tokens, and personal data.
Regenerate or audit documentation whenever routes, authentication, or response formats change.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated docs without checking dynamically registered routes.
Do not treat example server URLs as production configuration.
Do not include real credentials, session tokens, or private customer data in examples.

faq.question: Which API formats does this skill create?
faq.answer: It focuses on OpenAPI 3.0 and Swagger-style REST API documentation.

faq.question: Which frameworks are covered?
faq.answer: The instructions cover Express, FastAPI, Flask, NestJS, and Rails patterns.

faq.question: Can it update an existing OpenAPI file?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare route files with an existing specification and fill missing documentation.

faq.question: Does it verify live API behavior?
faq.answer: No. It reads source files and examples, but it does not run servers or make live requests.

faq.question: Can it infer authentication requirements?
faq.answer: It can document authentication hints found in middleware, decorators, comments, or existing configuration.

faq.question: What should users review after generation?
faq.answer: Users should review schemas, examples, security requirements, server URLs, and dynamically registered routes.
